lens: Deal flow roars back to open the week on a risk-on tape (Nasdaq +1.4%, S&P +0.9%): Magnolia's $4.06B buy of WildFire Energy (JPMorgan, Moelis, Citi buy-side; Jefferies, BofA sell-side), Intersnack's ~$2.9B take-private of Utz (Citi, RBC; BofA), Reformation's ~$239M IPO launch (JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley; Citi, RBC), and the Tempus/Personalis merger filing (Morgan Stanley; Centerview, TD Cowen).

stories.headline: JPMorgan, Moelis and Citi advise Magnolia Oil & Gas on its $4.06B acquisition of WildFire Energy
stories.why_it_matters: Magnolia Oil & Gas agreed to acquire Eagle Ford producer WildFire Energy for $4.06B from Warburg Pincus and Kayne Anderson. The release names J.P. Morgan and Moelis as lead financial advisers and Citi as a financial adviser to Magnolia (buyer), with JPMorgan, Citi and Wells Fargo providing committed financing. A large PE-backed upstream carve-out with reserves, title, and financing diligence running in parallel.
stories.suggested_action: Engage JPMorgan's energy coverage on Magnolia/WildFire — a $4B upstream acquisition with a financing syndicate is a heavy multi-party diligence use case.

deal_clock.event: Magnolia agrees to acquire WildFire Energy for $4.06B

stories.headline: Jefferies and BofA advise WildFire Energy on its $4.06B sale to Magnolia Oil & Gas
stories.why_it_matters: On the sell-side of the Magnolia/WildFire deal, the release names Jefferies as lead financial adviser and BofA Securities as a financial adviser to WildFire, a Warburg Pincus/Kayne Anderson-backed Eagle Ford producer. A sponsor exit of this scale runs a controlled, document-heavy sell-side diligence process.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Jefferies's energy/sponsor coverage on the WildFire sale — a $4B PE exit is a concrete near-term diligence conversation.

deal_clock.event: WildFire Energy agrees to $4.06B sale to Magnolia

stories.headline: Citi (lead, Special Committee) and RBC advise Utz on its ~$2.9B take-private by Intersnack
stories.why_it_matters: Intersnack Group agreed to take Utz Brands private at $14.25/share (~$2.9B EV). The release names Citi as exclusive financial adviser to Utz's Special Committee and RBC Capital Markets as a financial adviser to Utz, with BofA Securities advising Intersnack and providing committed debt financing. A public-company take-private with a Special Committee — the highest-diligence room type.
stories.suggested_action: Open with Citi's consumer coverage on the Utz take-private — a Special Committee process with fairness backup and a debt syndicate is a strong secure-diligence use case.

deal_clock.event: Intersnack agrees to take Utz private at $14.25/share

stories.headline: JPMorgan and Morgan Stanley lead Reformation's ~$239M NYSE IPO (Citi, RBC bookrunners)
stories.why_it_matters: Womenswear retailer Reformation launched its IPO (14.06M shares, $15–$17 range, up to ~$239M) on the NYSE under ticker REF. Reporting names J.P. Morgan and Morgan Stanley as joint lead bookrunners, with Citigroup and RBC Capital Markets as joint bookrunners. An active roadshow means live S-1 and due-diligence-defense workstreams.
stories.suggested_action: Use the Reformation launch as an ECM hook with Morgan Stanley's syndicate desk — live IPOs run coordinated diligence-defense data rooms across bookrunners and selling holders.

deal_clock.event: Reformation launches IPO, $15–$17 range

stories.headline: Guggenheim, Jefferies and TD Cowen advise on Freenome's ~$310M SPAC/PIPE public debut
stories.why_it_matters: Blood-based cancer-detection firm Freenome began trading (Nasdaq: FRNM) via a PCSC SPAC combination plus a $240M PIPE (~$310M gross). The release names Guggenheim Securities as capital-markets adviser and TD Cowen as lead financial adviser to Freenome, with Jefferies as lead financial/capital-markets adviser and joint lead PIPE placement agent for PCSC. A de-SPAC plus large PIPE is document-intensive across subscription and diligence workstreams.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Guggenheim on the Freenome debut — de-SPAC/PIPE combinations run heavy subscription-doc and diligence exchange that map directly to secure rooms.

deal_clock.event: Freenome debuts via PCSC SPAC + $240M PIPE
FRNM begins trading

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley advises Tempus on its acquisition of Personalis; Centerview and TD Cowen advise Personalis ($16.25/share)
stories.why_it_matters: Tempus AI agreed to acquire Personalis in a stock-for-stock two-step merger with an optional cash election up to 50% at $16.25/share. The S-4/425 names Morgan Stanley as financial adviser to Tempus (buyer) and Centerview Partners and TD Securities ("TD Cowen") as advisers to Personalis's board, each delivering fairness opinions. A diagnostics combination now advancing through the S-4/vote phase.
stories.suggested_action: Engage Morgan Stanley's healthcare-tech coverage on Tempus/Personalis — a filed stock-for-stock merger with a cash election runs two-sided diligence into the vote.

deal_clock.event: Tempus/Personalis merger materials (425) filed; $16.25/share cash election

stories.headline: Guggenheim advises the Destination XL board on a stock-for-stock merger with FULLBEAUTY Brands
stories.why_it_matters: Destination XL (DXLG) filed a preliminary proxy for a stock-for-stock merger with Oaktree-backed FULLBEAUTY Brands (~55/45 in FBB's favor). The proxy names Guggenheim Securities as financial adviser to the DXL board — notably, the board is now recommending against the share-issuance proposal, keeping the process contested and diligence-heavy. (BofA appears only as ABL lender; not an M&A adviser.)
stories.suggested_action: A contested stock-for-stock merger with a board recommending against is a live, document-intensive situation — a reason to talk to Guggenheim on secure, auditable diligence access.

deal_clock.event: DXL files preliminary merger proxy (PREM14A)

stories.headline: Raymond James advises Identiv on the $50M sale of its IoT business to Trackonomy
stories.why_it_matters: Identiv (INVE) filed a preliminary proxy for the sale of its IoT business (plus $25M cash) to Trackonomy Systems for $50M of Trackonomy Series C preferred, pivoting to a "Physical AI" strategy. The proxy names Raymond James as financial adviser to Identiv's board, delivering the fairness opinion. A carve-out asset sale with valuation and closing diligence.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Raymond James on the Identiv carve-out — an asset sale for stock consideration runs focused diligence and closing-document exchange.

deal_clock.event: Identiv files preliminary proxy for IoT-business sale

stories.headline: TD Cowen advises Searchlight and Abry on the completed ~$726M take-private of KORE Group
stories.why_it_matters: Searchlight Capital and Abry Partners completed their ~$726M take-private of IoT-connectivity firm KORE Group, which is now private. The release names TD Cowen as financial adviser to Searchlight and Abry (Rothschild advised KORE's Special Committee). A completed take-private runs delisting, closing, and sponsor-onboarding workstreams through shared rooms.
stories.suggested_action: Congratulate TD Cowen on the closed KORE take-private and ask about the next sponsor mandate where fast, clean room setup helps at signing and close.

deal_clock.event: KORE completes take-private; becomes private company

stories.headline: BofA advises Cross Country Healthcare on its completed take-private by Knox Lane
stories.why_it_matters: PE firm Knox Lane completed its take-private of Cross Country Healthcare, with a simultaneous carve-out of the locums business to All Star Healthcare. The release names BofA Securities as exclusive financial adviser to Cross Country Healthcare (MTS Health Partners advised Knox Lane). A take-private plus divisional carve-out means multi-entity diligence and closing-document exchange.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to BofA's healthcare-services coverage on the Cross Country close — a take-private with a simultaneous carve-out is a multi-entity room use case.

deal_clock.event: Knox Lane completes Cross Country Healthcare take-private

stories.headline: Risk-on rally: Nasdaq +1.4%, S&P +0.9%, Dow +1.0% as VIX falls to ~17
stories.why_it_matters: US equities rallied broadly on Jul 21, with the Nasdaq up 1.4% (25,857) and the S&P 500 up 0.9% (7,513); the VIX fell ~8% to 17.1 and WTI firmed to ~$84. Memory/semiconductor names led and advisory stocks rallied with the tape (GS +2.8%, MS +2.9%, EVR +2.8%). A constructive tape is the backdrop against which fresh mandates — Magnolia/WildFire, Utz, Reformation's IPO — get signed and launched.
stories.suggested_action: Frame outreach around a risk-on open supporting fresh signings and launches — a natural reason to talk room readiness while deal flow accelerates.

opportunities.headline: Adviser to Tempus on its stock-for-stock acquisition of Personalis ($16.25/share cash election)
opportunities.why_it_matters: Morgan Stanley advises Tempus AI on its signed stock-for-stock acquisition of Personalis, with an optional cash election up to 50% at $16.25/share (Centerview and TD Cowen advise Personalis). Merger materials (Form 425) filed Jul 20 — a diagnostics combination advancing through the S-4/vote phase with two-sided diligence.

deal_clock.event: Tempus/Personalis merger materials filed; $16.25/share cash election

opportunities.outreach_idea: Open on Tempus/Personalis — a filed stock-for-stock diagnostics merger is a concrete reason to engage Morgan Stanley's healthcare-tech team on secure diligence.

outreach_draft.subject: Tempus / Personalis — supporting a filed diagnostics merger
outreach_draft.body:
Congratulations on Morgan Stanley's role advising Tempus on the Personalis combination. Stock-for-stock mergers with cash elections run two-sided diligence through the S-4 and shareholder vote — would welcome a conversation on how we support secure, auditable data-room access to close.

opportunities.headline: Adviser to First Hawaiian on its all-stock acquisition of TriCo Bancshares
opportunities.why_it_matters: Evercore advises First Hawaiian on its all-stock acquisition of TriCo Bancshares — now under a definitive merger agreement filed Jul 15 (KBW/Stifel advises TriCo). A signed public bank merger triggers two-sided diligence and integration planning into the shareholder vote.

deal_clock.event: First Hawaiian/TriCo combination announced
Definitive merger agreement filed

opportunities.outreach_idea: Open on the now-definitive First Hawaiian/TriCo agreement — a signed bank merger is a concrete reason to engage Evercore's FIG team on diligence infrastructure.

outreach_draft.subject: First Hawaiian / TriCo — supporting a signed bank merger
outreach_draft.body:
Congratulations on Evercore's role advising First Hawaiian on the TriCo Bancshares combination, now under a definitive agreement. All-stock bank mergers spin up two-sided diligence and integration workstreams heading into the vote — would welcome a conversation on how we support clean, auditable data-room access.

market_snapshot.macro_note: Broad risk-on rally on Jul 21: S&P +0.9%, Nasdaq +1.4%, Dow +1.0%, with the VIX down ~8% to 17.1. Memory/semis led (Micron, Western Digital, Seagate double-digit gains); advisory names rallied with the tape (GS +2.8%, MS +2.9%, EVR +2.8%). Values are live intraday levels from the Jul 21 session (~1pm ET), not final closes; JEF and several boutique quotes omitted where only stale or single-source prints were verifiable — accuracy over completeness.

talking_point.quote: Deal flow came back hot to start the week — Magnolia's $4B WildFire buy, Utz going private, Reformation's IPO launch, and Tempus/Personalis filing — all on a risk-on tape with the VIX back to 17. Fresh signings and launches spin up diligence clocks immediately. How are you thinking about room readiness across the processes you're launching this quarter?
talking_point.use_with: M&A, sponsor, and ECM coverage teams (esp. JPMorgan, Jefferies, Citi, Morgan Stanley, Moelis, Guggenheim)
talking_point.context: Anchors on a busy Jul 21 deal tape against a rallying market — a natural way to open a room-readiness conversation as signings and launches accelerate.

ranked.read: Magnolia's $4.06B WildFire Energy buy (JPMorgan, Moelis, Citi buy-side; Jefferies, BofA sell-side) headlines a large PE-backed upstream carve-out with a financing syndicate.
Intersnack takes Utz private at $14.25/share (~$2.9B; Citi lead to the Special Committee, RBC to Utz, BofA to Intersnack) — a Special Committee process with debt financing.
Tempus/Personalis files its stock-for-stock merger (Morgan Stanley; Centerview, TD Cowen) and Freenome debuts via SPAC+$240M PIPE (Guggenheim, TD Cowen, Jefferies).
Reformation launches its ~$239M NYSE IPO (JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley lead; Citi, RBC) — a live roadshow with a four-bank syndicate.

pipeline_watch.note: Named covered-bank mandates and sector heat. Rumored / no-adviser situations live in the Rumor Mill.

skeptics_corner.note: Unconfirmed leads — rumored deals and situations with no adviser named yet (or advisers unconfirmed). Real reporting, but treat as speculative: chase them, don't quote them on a call.

items.lead: The retirement/insurance group is reportedly fielding takeover interest — Principal Financial approached earlier in 2026 — with activist Toms Capital among its largest holders adding pressure.
items.why_unverified: Semafor report of interest only; no active talks confirmed, company has not commented, and the earlier Principal approach reportedly went nowhere.

items.lead: The payments-software firm is exploring a sale of its Biller/billing division, valued around $1.5B, amid strong demand for recurring-revenue payments assets.
items.why_unverified: Reuters sourced it to people familiar; early-stage "exploring," no adviser or buyer named.

items.lead: Activist Barington Capital is ramping up a campaign pushing the VITAS hospice / Roto-Rooter parent for a strategic review and cost cuts, having taken a stake and proposed a director.
items.why_unverified: Activist push, not a company-sanctioned process — Chemed has not committed to any review or sale.

items.lead: The AI-model-routing startup (recent ~$1.3B valuation, Alphabet-backed) has reportedly discussed a potential multibillion-dollar sale to a larger tech company.
items.why_unverified: The Information reports preliminary interest; no acquirer named, private company, no confirmation.
